title: 'You let BJP, Cong speak, but not us': Akhilesh erupts in LS over NEET discussion
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav accused the Lok Sabha Speaker of unfair treatment. He stated his party was denied a chance to speak on the NEET paper leak. The government and Congress were allowed to present their views while his party was not. Yadav insisted the controversy concerned students and their future, not just politics. He defended protests outside the Prime Minister's residence due to the lack of House discussion.

title: Traffic jam! BCCI CoE braces for an influx of contracted, targeted India players
content: The BCCI Centre of Excellence is set for a hectic week as nearly a dozen contracted and targeted India players undergo fitness assessments and rehabilitation ahead of the Sri Lanka Tests. With injuries mounting and a packed international calendar looming, the team management is keen to ensure a fit and ready player pool.

title: Trump’s 200% tariffs on pharma: Why India’s generic medicines could still remain competitive
content: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced the final stage of his pharmaceutical tariff plan, under which imported generic medicines will continue to enter the US without duties until August 1, 2028.

title: Meta's 'highest-paid' employee taunts Google’s AI model, says ‘Gemini who’
content: Meta's Alexandr Wang taunted Google's Gemini model after a leaderboard showed Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 performing better.  Google recently launched Gemini 3.6 Flash, claiming improved efficiency and quality over its predecessor.  Gemini 3.6 Flash shows higher precision in coding and better computer use capabilities.  It also outperforms the previous model in knowledge work and multimodal tasks.  This development highlights the ongoing competition in the artificial intelligence sector.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: Jana Nayagan: Vijay’s final film as actor, first as CM — cast, salaries, bookings & buzz
content: Vijay's political action film 'Jana Nayagan' is releasing on July 23 after significant anticipation.  The movie boasts a strong ensemble cast and a massive production budget.  Director H. Vinoth has extensively reworked the plot for local audiences and political themes.  The film faced censor delays and legal battles before securing an 'A' certificate.  Advance bookings are strong, with trade experts expecting a surge before its release.

title: 11-year-old girl dies in River Thames after asking dad to dip her toes
content: In a tragic incident, an 11-year-old girl lost her life after she innocently asked to dip her toes in the River Thames. Two weeks of extensive searching led to the recovery of her body. The inquest determined her death was accidental, resulting from immersion in frigid waters, with no signs of foul play among peers. This devastating loss has left her family heartbroken and in mourning.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Hollywood Hills to Malibu beachfront: Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million dollar properties
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: PCB makes padel mandatory for Pakistan cricketers at National Cricket Academy
content: The Pakistan Cricket Board has made padel a mandatory part of training at the National Cricket Academy, with players required to play 40-minute sessions every alternate day. The move aims to improve fitness, agility and fielding. The PCB has also added a padel court, installed an AI bowling machine, and plans to build basketball and indoor sports facilities.

title: ‘Muslim name hurting house hunt’: Woman moves HC for ‘ghar waapsi’
content: A Vadodara woman seeks to revert to her Christian maiden name after facing housing challenges. She adopted a Muslim name after marrying a Muslim man, which now causes social issues. The Gujarat High Court has issued notices to state authorities regarding her name change request. Authorities previously denied her gazette publication without a divorce decree. The court is now seeking replies on why her request was not accepted.

title: 'Don't want to watch videos': SC declines urgent listing of plea on Delhi protest crackdown
content: SC declined an urgent hearing on alleged police brutality against students.  Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding the education minister's resignation over exam irregularities. Security forces used lathis and tear gas to disperse the demonstrators.  The CJP's sit-in protest at Jantar Mantar continued for its second month. Sonam Wangchuk remains on an indefinite hunger strike for accountability.

title: Why several fertiliser vessels are stranded at Indian ports during kharif sowing season
content: Congestion has affected several major ports, including Kandla, Mundra, Jawaharlal Nehru Port (Nhava Sheva) and Chennai. Industry officials said that the situation has been aggravated by the rerouting of vessels due to geopolitical tensions.

title: Bandar Abbas at US-Iran conflict: Why this city is important for both sides
content: US forces are targeting Iran's southern port city of Bandar Abbas.  This campaign aims to degrade Iran's ability to threaten shipping.  Bandar Abbas is crucial for Iran's maritime logistics and coastal defense.  Disruptions there impact global energy markets and international trade.  The conflict escalates tensions over vital energy corridor control.

title: What happened to Charleston White? YouTuber shot at Houston party
content: Charleston White says he was shot at during a visit to Houston's Fifth Ward after a group allegedly confronted him over past remarks and asked him to apologize. In a livestream, the comedian confirmed he was not injured, shared his version of the incident, and said he plans to file a report with the FBI.

title: Quote of the day by Priyanka Chopra
content: Priyanka Chopra highlights the importance of listening to children for their confidence.  She believes validating young children's feelings fosters self-assurance and emotional growth.  Chopra's career spans Bollywood and Hollywood, achieving international recognition.  She also advocates for women's rights and education globally.  The actress and her husband welcomed their daughter Malti Marie in 2022.

title: Hyderabad: Airline crew member 'raped' by cab driver on way to airport
content: A woman cabin crew member alleged rape by a cab driver on July 20. The incident occurred near the airport at an isolated construction site. Police registered a rape case and arrested the 23-year-old accused driver. He was apprehended at his residence and sent to judicial remand. The victim was provided with counseling and medical examination after the incident.

title: Tankers carrying Saudi crude for India, China make a U-turn in Red Sea on Houthi threats
content: On Monday, the Houthis announced a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening another front in the US-Israeli conflict with Iran and increasing risks to global energy supplies and international trade beyond the Gulf region.

title: Aamir Khan quits Ashneer Grover biopic over creative differences: Report
content: Aamir Khan has reportedly exited the Ashneer Grover biopic project. Creative differences with director Rahul Mody led to his departure from the film. Shraddha Kapoor remains committed to the project as an actor and producer. The filmmakers are now searching for a younger actor to play Ashneer Grover. Production is expected to commence after the lead role is finalized soon.

title: Paytm ordered to pay Rs 21,437 for failing to refund cancelled flight ticket
content: A consumer commission ordered Paytm to refund a cancelled flight ticket.  The airline had already sent the refund amount to Paytm.  Paytm failed to ensure the customer received the money.  The commission found Paytm liable for poor service and deficiency.  Paytm must now pay the ticket amount, compensation, and costs.

title: 'Chaar mahine mein kaafi ups and downs aaye': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns to Harare for his senior India debut after U-19 World Cup triumph. He hopes to create new memories at the venue where he achieved past success. Sooryavanshi aims to bounce back after a challenging maiden outing in senior colours. He believes his knowledge of the Harare conditions will be beneficial. India faces Zimbabwe after recent T20I series losses in Ireland and England.

title: World's 'happiest' nation is facing youth exodus crisis: Here's its unique solution
content: Bhutan, known for its unique happiness philosophy, is experiencing a troubling trend of youth emigration. In response, the government is introducing Gelephu Mindfulness City, which aims to seamlessly integrate economic advancement with cultural preservation and ecological sustainability. Supported by India through infrastructure development and rail connectivity, this initiative hopes to inspire young Bhutanese to choose to stay and thrive in their country.

title: Bombay HC grants Rs 20,000 maintenance to educated, unemployed wife
content: The Bombay High Court enhanced a wife's monthly maintenance payment. An educated wife cannot be denied support simply for possessing qualifications. The court found no proof the wife quit her job to claim maintenance. The husband's claims of her employment were unsubstantiated by evidence. The court considered the husband's income and the child's expenses.

title: Meenakshi seshadri to reunite with jackie shroff after 40 years for OTT comeback
content: Meenakshi Seshadri marks her acting comeback with Prime Video series 'Nanavati vs Nanavati', reuniting with Jackie Shroff after four decades. The eight-episode show, directed by Vipul Shah, also stars Bhagyashree, Ram Kapoor, and Anupriya Goenka, and is being shot in Andheri West and Powai. Official announcement awaited.

title: Another oil shock loading for India? How Red Sea route disruptions may hit supply
content: Iran-backed Yemeni group, the Houthis, have threatened a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. What this essentially means is that Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports are set to be hit. And India which gets a big portion of its crude imports from the Middle East country.

title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul over PM residence march
content: The BJP launched a sharp political counteroffensive against Rahul Gandhi's protest.  Senior BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad criticized Gandhi's actions as irresponsible and condemnable.  The BJP announced nationwide protests outside Congress offices in response to the demonstration.  Prasad defended police action and highlighted government efforts on exam integrity.  He assured that those involved in exam irregularities would face trial and punishment.

title: Canada joins European 6th-generation GCAP fighter program, will India be next?
content: Canada joins the sixth-generation Global Combat Air Programme as an observer.  The United States is developing its F-47 fighter under the Next Generation Air Dominance project.  China is already testing two separate sixth-generation platforms, the J-36 and J-50.  India is working on its Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft, expected by 2035.  These advanced aircraft will integrate artificial intelligence and operate unmanned platforms.

title: France's top General Fabien Mandon visits India to deepen strategic partnership
content: France's chief of defence staff visited India to further strengthen strategic ties. High-level visits and joint exercises underscore growing defense cooperation between the nations. Recent agreements include Rafale jet deliveries and helicopter assembly lines in India. Both countries prioritize self-reliance and strategic autonomy in their defense partnerships. This collaboration spans military, industrial, and strategic domains, reinforcing a trusted relationship.

title: 'Watched murder conspiracy films': Siya-Chetan 'researched' different ways to kill Ketan
content: Accused researched murder methods and locations before killing Ketan Agarwal.  Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary allegedly planned the murder meticulously.  They surveyed multiple sites, choosing Lohagad Fort for the crime.  The duo pushed Agarwal off a cliff, making his death appear accidental.  The investigation into this alleged murder conspiracy is ongoing.

title: Katrina Kaif faces backlash over silence on CJP protests as fan page shuts down
content: Katrina Kaif faces backlash from fans over her silence on CJP protests, with fan page 'katholic_fans' shutting down, calling her out for lacking "the spine to speak for Indians." While some fans defended her right to choose, celebs like Shabana Azmi, Preity Zinta, and Sonu Sood have voiced support.

title: 'Didn't bring sarees for me': Thane woman strangles husband
content: A domestic dispute over sarees allegedly led to a brutal murder in Badlapur East. A 30-year-old woman was arrested after her husband's death was initially reported as a heart attack. Investigators uncovered inconsistencies in her account, prompting a deeper inquiry into the incident. The post-mortem examination revealed injuries inconsistent with natural causes, confirming foul play.

title: Relationship ending without marriage doesn't amount to rape: Uttarakhand HC
content: The Uttarakhand high court quashed a rape case registered against a man.  The court found the relationship began consensually through the Tinder dating app.  A physical relationship developed between the two individuals over several months.  The court ruled a breakdown of the relationship did not constitute rape.  Further criminal proceedings were deemed an abuse of legal process.

title: 'English cricket more important than IPL': Cook reignites Bethell debate
content: Alastair Cook has reignited the Jacob Bethell IPL debate, insisting England should always come before franchise cricket. His remarks resurfaced after Bethell's knee injury ruled him out of The Hundred and cast doubt on his Pakistan Test availability. Cook questioned the value of Bethell sitting on the IPL bench, though the youngster previously defended his decision to stay at RCB.

title: 0%, 100%, then 200%: How Trump's new generic drug tariff plan could hit India
content: Donald Trump announced phased tariffs on imported generic medicines starting August 2026.  These tariffs will gradually increase to 200 percent over three years.  This policy aims to reshore pharmaceutical production back into America.  India, a major supplier of affordable generics, faces significant impact from these changes.  The move adds uncertainty to ongoing bilateral trade agreement.

title: Cockroach Janta Party, BJP, Congress And The Tale Of Three Protests
content: The Cockroach Janta Party is leading a students' protest over alleged NEET leak, while the BJP and Congress are holding a nationwide protest against each other.

title: Kedarnath Yatra Halted After Landslide Blocks Trek Route
content: The landslide occurred near Chhodi, about one kilometre beyond Gaurikund on the Kedarnath trek route, blocking the path with rocks and debris.

title: Kimi K3 vs OpenAI: Is China Really Beating The US In AI Race?
content: In its own launch notes, Moonshot AI has admitted Kimi K3 still trails OpenAI's newest model and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 on overall performance.

title: Mamdani Realises He Can't Legally Arrest Netanyahu, Urges US To Enforce Warrant
content: The New York City mayor said that the city lacks the authority to enforce the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.

title: OpenAI's Agents Reach 10 Million Users After ChatGPT Work Debut
content: Combined usage of Codex and Chat GPT Work nearly doubled since earlier this month, as Open AI pushes to develop advanced AI agents.

title: Opinion: Opinion | A Rs 5,000 Fine Can't Fix India's 'Babu' Raj. But It's A Start
content: A new bill in Delhi imposes a fine of Rs 250 a day on bureaucrats, with a Rs 5,000 ceiling, if their service for citizens is delayed without a valid justification. Can this fix India's great 'Babu' bottleneck?

title: 'He Didn't Stop While Convicted': Epstein Survivor Recalls Years Of Abuse
content: At the centre of Roza Gilles story is Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 plea deal in Florida for charges including soliciting a minor for prostitution.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates:  Government Ready For NEET Paper Leak Debate In Lok Sabha, Opposition Seeks Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
content: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates: The opposition has been demanding a discussion on the NEET paper leak issue and is also seeking the resignation of Pradhan over the handling of the controversy over the alleged examination irregularities.

title: Trump Threatens To Bomb Iran's Pickaxe Nuke Site Soon. But Can US Destroy It?
content: The warning came even as it remains unclear just what has been placed at Pickaxe Mountain, as the United Nations' nuclear watchdog has never gained access to the site.

title: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Centre To Meet CJP Delegation For The Second Time Today
content: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: The huge protest over the NEET paper leak are continuing for the third straight day today.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 750 Points As Oil Advances Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude hits $91 as US announces new round of strikes against Iran.

title: Abhishek Banerjee's Legal Notice To Rebel Who Called Him "Criminal, Murderer"
content: According to the legal notice shared by the All India Trinamool Congress (AITC), Madan Mitra allegedly made remarks accusing Abhishek Banerjee of criminal activities, including allegations of involvement in killings and hiring contract killers.

title: Woman, 21, Injured At Delhi CJP Protest Taken Off Ventilator, Is Stable: Doctors
content: The woman is fully conscious, the hospital confirmed. She is responding to the doctors' instructions and breathing on her own.

title: India's 5.5% Unemployment Rate Is Better Than Many Economies. But There's A Catch
content: India adds roughly 19-22 lakh net payroll subscribers every month, with nearly 60 per cent of new members belonging to the 18-25 age group.

title: 'Come To Us If You Want Talks': CJP's Message To Centre Amid Protests
content: Abhijeet Dipke, the founder of the Cockroach Janta Party, a satirical movement that began two months ago, has made it clear: the government has to take the lead.

title: Renowned Indian-Origin Scientist Quits US Lab After Sexual Misconduct Probe
content: It was at least the second time in recent years that Salk has investigated Satchidananda Panda following harassment allegations.

title: Trying To Mislead Muslims: VD Satheesan Slams Pinarayi Vijayan Over Waqf Row
content: "The opposition leader, in order to spread communalism, has wrongly carried out communal propaganda to turn Muslims against the UDF and this government," the Chief Minister said.

title: 'What and how much can courts do': SC reluctant to take up PIL on paper leaks
content: the bench adjourned the hearing to next month, observing that it was already seized of the NEET-UG paper leak case . the court was hearing a petition by advocate Ashwini Upadhyay, who said repeated paper leaks leave students in dire straits .

title: Pakistan asks US for $10bn lifeline to shore up struggling economy: Report
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: J&K: Terrorists kill Amarnath Yatra security personnel in Anantnag attack
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 3 LIVE: Government says ready to discuss paper leak and other issues; Pradhan must resign first, says Kharge
content: Union Ministers Kiren Rijiju and J.P. Nadda said that the government was ready to discuss paper leaks and other issues in Parliament; Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge says that Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan must resign for a fair discussion to take place; logjam continues and repeated adjournments rock both Houses

title: Nothing can shame people unfazed by Ram temple donation theft: Allahabad High Court
content: Justice Sreedharan simultaneously criticised the systemic corruption plaguing Indian institutions, noting that the recent “theft” of donations at the Ram temple represents the “nadir of Indians’ integrity”

title: Mylatti BESS project to be commissioned in October, says Power Minister Sunny Joseph
content: The Minister directed officials and implementing agencies to ensure the projects are completed within the scheduled time frame

title: Abolition of NEET only solution to students’ issues: Stalin
content: He said that more than any other demand, NEET abolition should be at the heart of the student protests in Delhi

title: Before ‘Jana Nayagan’: When an 18-year-old Vijay walked the halls of Loyola College
content: Vijay’s maiden venture as a solo hero, Naalaiya Theerpu, helmed by S.A. Chandrasekhar, did not set the box office on fire; even as the audience response remained muted, the actor was noticed, and he won the Best New Face Actor award from Cinema Express

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: CJP says ready to meet Nadda at 'neutral venue' today; 10 FIRs registered over July 20 march so far
content: Seven Oppn. MPs to visit Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta hospital to urge him to break his fast; 16 Delhi Metro stations closed over 'security reasons'

title: Kerala BDS student death: HC directs investigating officer, special court to produce documents in Dr. Ram’s arrest
content: HC observes that 'serious allegations’ have been raised against investigating officer in the release of the accused and expresses suspicion that top officials were involved in it to ‘save’ Dr. Ram from detention

title: Dharmendra Pradhan meets Birla as government agrees to discuss NEET paper leak in Lok Sabha
content: The Opposition has been demanding a discussion on the NEET paper leak issue and is also seeking the resignation of Mr. Pradhan over the handling of the controversy over the alleged examination irregularities

title: Kafir screenshot case in Kerala: SIT names DYFI leader Ribesh Ramakrishnan as an accused
content: Police sources say notice will be issued soon directing him to appear for interrogation and that further legal action will be taken in compliance with court’s anticipatory bail order

title: Delhi Internet shutdown during CJP protest: Where does the law stand? | Explained
content: Legal questions arise about government authority and public safety measures following Delhi’s Internet shutdown during the CJP protest

title: Monsoon session: Sharad Pawar, Supriya Sule meet PM Modi in Parliament
content: Both Sharad Pawar and MP Supriya Sule had earlier joined the protestors at Jantar Mantar on July 21, urging the Centre to ‘sensitively consider the demands’ and take appropriate action

title: Tensions escalate in Vijayawada as BJP activists march to Congress office over NEET protests
content: BJP workers led by State president P.V.N. Madhav gathered outside Andhra Ratna Bhavan on Wednesday against the Congress’s Delhi sit-in over the alleged NEET-UG leak; the party alleged that three of its district leaders were injured, and the police detained some activists

title: Pedestrians welcome Shivajinagar footpath clearance, street vendors deny link to attack on GBA official
content: The Street Vendors’ Joint Action Committee condemned the attack on the officials and said it was a deliberate attempt by some people to link it with street vendors

title: SC directs Centre, MP to comply with order on namaz site near Bhojshala complex ‘in letter and spirit’
content: The dispute concerns the Bhojshala Temple-Kamal Maula Mosque complex in Madhya Pradesh’s Dhar district, where both Hindu and Muslim communities have long asserted competing religious claims

title: Sharad Pawar slams Centre over ‘irresponsible’ handling of Wangchuk's hunger strike
content: Mr. Pawar asserted that while the government moved to hospitalise Mr. Wangchuk as the situation became "uncontrollable", the protest would continue regardless of the action against the activist

title: Bhojshala complex case: Supreme Court suggests temporary prayer space for Muslims until case decided
content: The Bench further prohibited the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) from allowing any structural alterations to be made to the disputed structure without the prior permission of the apex court.

title: Telangana CM Revanth steps up attack on Rahul Gandhi’s detention; leaders and cadre stage dharnas
content: Telangana Congress cadre and leaders participate in Chalo Raj Bhavan led by TPCC president Mahesh Goud. Ponnam Prabhakar and Jagga Reddy stage dharnas in Husnabad and Sangareddy respectively. Deputy CM protests in Hanamkonda.

title: BLOs should not insist on hardcopies if Enumeration Forms were submitted online, says Telangana CEO
content: BLOs should also not insist on Aadhaar cards for submission of EFs, says Telangana Chief Electoral Officer

title: Kerala CM says former CPI(M)-led LDF govt had agreed to appoint non-Muslim members on Waqf Board
content: V.D. Satheesan alleges that Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan is trying to ‘foment communal sentiments' against Congress-led UDF govt

title: Chittoor police arrest 42-year-old in POCSO case within a day
content: The Superintendent of Police said special teams traced the accused within a day of the case being registered; he has been remanded in judicial custody

title: World is ‘extremely turbulent’, Jaishankar calls for deeper India-ASEAN cooperation
content: India-ASEAN agenda covers trade and investment, mobility and talent, tech, digital and AI, green and sustainability, as well as connectivity, Jaishankar said

title: ‘Don’t waste our time’: CJI Kant declines to take cognisance of police violence during CJP’s Parliament march
content: “We are not interested in videos. We don’t have time to watch them,” the Chief Justice orally remarked, declining to take cognisance of alleged videographic evidence of police violence on student protestors

title: INDIA bloc parties marching separately but striking together
content: The parties put into practice the Trotskyist principle of “March separately, strike together!” a line that late CPI(M) General Secretary Sitaram Yechury often used to repeat when talking about opposition unity

title: Hyderabad airport suggests passengers to plan commute in advance amid Telangana-wide strike by app-based cab, auto services
content: Passengers were asked to consider prepaid taxis, car rentals, Pushpak airport liner and other alternative transportation options at Hyderabad airport

title: Union Ministers Nadda, Jitendra Singh meet Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram
content: Both J.P. Nadda and Jitendra Singh spent over an hour with Sonam Wangchuk at the hospital enquiring about his health, and conveyed the government’s decision to initiate a debate in Parliament

title: Kochi youth hold midnight vigil in solidarity with Jantar Mantar protests
content: Protestors, many clad in black, join candlelight vigil following a call made through an Instagram handle named Azadi Kerala

title: Pinarayi Vijayan condemns arrest of Rahul Gandhi in Delhi, says Centre ‘suppressing’ protest using force
content: In a Facebook post, Pinarayi says that according to news reports, ‘a gang of thugs with lathis’ were attacking protesters along with the police

title: Rebel Trinamool faction is BJP’s B-team to keep Suvendu in power: Mamata
content: Rebels accuse Mamata Banerjee of milking the martyrs’ sacrifice for her political gains while favouring her own family over others

title: What past patterns and current concerns indicate about the upcoming bypolls
content: The byelections to three seats — Bankipur in Bihar, Daita in Madhya Pradesh and Manjalpur in Gujarat — are now under the spotlight; while trends from the past five years indicate an advantage for the ruling party, these constituencies have been drawing attention for a few other reasons

title: When India acted tough with Sri Lankan Tamil leaders Anton Balasingham, Chandrahasan in 1985
content: In August 1985, Anton S. Balasingham, who became the best-known international face of the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE), and S.C. Chandrahasan, founder of Organisation for Eelam Refugees Rehabilitation (OfERR) were served orders to leave India within 48 hours.

title: Gujarat approves de-addiction centres in all district hospitals
content: According to the government, each centre will provide scientific detoxification, de-addiction therapy, mental healthcare, family counselling and rehabilitation services

title: Watch: Director H. Vinoth interview | On ‘Jana Nayagan’ and Vijay’s support during testing times
content: Filmmaker H. Vinoth speaks about his upcoming film Jana Nayagan, starring Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay, the unwavering support he received from the star during the turbulent months the film endured, what success means for a politically charged film that unfortunately leaked online, and more.

title: Kerala MVD officer transferred after penalising police vehicle in Wadakkanchery
content: Transport Minister C.P. John had said enforcement action against police vehicle was ‘a little excessive’

title: AMMA row: Filmmaker Vinayan challenges Shwetha Menon to push for govt probe into ‘power group' in Malayalam cinema
content: Vinayan asks whether Shwetha Menon is ready to 'initiate sincere action against the power group' as the members of her own association had opposed it earlier

title: SIT formed to look into allegations against Art of Living, says Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister
content: The Karnataka government has constituted a 11-member Special Investigation Team led by Bengaluru Regional Commissioner Amlan Aditya Biswas to probe the alleged grabbing of government land by the Art of Living ashram and its affiliate organisations on the outskirts of Bengaluru

title: Andhra Pradesh Congress condemns lathi-charge on students at Jantar Mantar
content: APCC vice-president Kolanukonda Shivaji said suppressing students’ voices went against the spirit of democracy, and sought a law to prevent paper leaks and a transparent examination system

title: BJP Kerala chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar says protests are democratic, but violence and anarchy are not
content: In a post on X, the BJP leader says: “Protests are a part of democracy and is a right of every citizen. Agreed.” He added, “Violent protests are not democracy. Anarchy is not democracy. Lawlessness is not democracy”

title: BIS seizes ₹10 crore worth of 7.875 kg non-hallmarked gold jewellery in Telangana’s Warangal raid
content: BIS Hyderabad Branch Office Director advises consumers to purchase only BIS-hallmarked gold jewellery carrying a valid HUID, verify the HUID through the BIS CARE mobile application before purchase and insist on obtaining a proper invoice

title: Odisha CM attributes policy driven reforms to State being bracketed among top five performers by NITI Aayog
content: The recognition validates Odisha’s sustained focus on governance reforms, ease of doing business, institutional efficiency and investor facilitation, he says

title: Bihar BJP delegation meets ECI, complains about Prashant Kishor’s expenditure in Bankipur byelection
content: The memorandum submitted by the BJP delegation raised concerns about the alleged violation of the prescribed election expenditure limit, spending funds through shell companies, and influencing voters through fake social media accounts and bot networks

title: U.P. districts conduct physical route inspection, review security for Kanwar Yatra
content: The Uttar Pradesh government has said it has pushed for zero incident, zero accident during the yatra

title: Karnataka steps up drive to reduce trans fats in food supply chain; over 45 lakh litres of used cooking oil collected since 2024
content: Officials pointed out that industrially produced trans fats, commonly found in partially hydrogenated oils used in some processed and fried foods, are among the most harmful dietary risk factors

title: Living by the sun, losing to the rain: life in Marakkanam’s salt pans in T.N.
content: For salt pan workers, the weather forms the basis of their livelihood: long, hot days take a toll on their body but allow them to earn, while spells of rain offer relief but mean wages are lost. Climate change is making their occupation increasingly precarious but help from the government remains limited

title: SWAYAM outlet for SHG women’s products sought at Bhogapuram airport
content: MSME Minister Kondapalli Srinivas seeks a dedicated retail outlet at Bhogapuram airport for SWAYAM brand products prepared by SHG women

title: Why are pellet guns used as a ‘non-lethal’ crowd-control measure? | Explained
content: They were last known to have been used during the 2024 farmers protest at Khanuari and Shambhu barrier along the Punjab-Haryana border. Though the police denied the use of pellet guns, farmer leaders had alleged then that several people were injured

title: UAS-B introduces low-cost technology to farmers to produce biochar, trains them to improve soil health
content: The team from UAS-B has used a simple and portable drum technology that doesn’t cost much to produce biochar

title: Study to evaluate impact of heat stress on health in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka
content: Participants will receive a digital watch to keep track of body temperature;  1,200 households roped in per site for the study; project to be taken up at Perungudi and Poompuhar in Tamil Nadu, and Sirwar and Chalakarre in Karnataka

title: Hospitals must not function as an extension of law enforcement: Safdarjung Hospital resident doctors
content: Residents association seeks President’s intervention in Wangchuk case; defending patients’ rights, doctors say hospitals must not be seen as places of detention or used to facilitate non-medical objective
